title: Dark Side Elite
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Dark side|more=Elite}} {{Organization |image=[[File:Dark_Empire_Emblem_Cyan.svg]] |name=Dark Side Elite |type= |founder=[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]][[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]] |leader=*[[Sedriss QL]]{{FactFile|85|SED 1-4|Exekutor Sedress}} German version *[[Xecr Nist]][[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]] |subgroup= |headquarters=*[[Byss/Legends|Byss]], [[Emperor's Citadel]] *[[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]], [[Bast Castle]][[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]] |locations= |formed= |founded= |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved=[[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]][[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' |restored= |affiliation=[[Dark Empire]] }} {{Quote|Let this power enter you and fill you with the ''knowledge'' and the ''strength'' of the dark side of the Force that is ''mine'' to give—!|Darth Sidious, empowering his Dark Jedi|}} The '''Dark Side Elite''', also known as the '''Dark Side Warriors''' and unofficially as the '''Emperor's Dark Jedi''', was a body of seven [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] established by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], also known as the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Sidious, during his resurrection in several [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] bodies. The elite group was hand selected by Sidious to lead [[Operation Shadow Hand]] in the [[Dark Empire]] quest to retake [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Sidious made sure that the group always contained seven warriors, and if one of their number was lost, he made sure to immediately appoint replacements. When a [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] was selected into the brotherhood, they went through an initiation of sorts in which the adept was empowered with the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by Sidious himself. As the war accelerated, the Dark Side Elite began to take a more personal role in the war and were tasked with hunting down [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to prevent his interference in the Emperor's plans. However, the Emperor's Dark Jedi were unable to match Skywalker's power, and with the help of other [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] that Skywalker had found on his journeys, along with his sister [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], the Elite were withered down to a paltry few. In fact, one of their number, [[Kam Solusar]], defected to Skywalker's side, and began to use their powers against them. With a high number of failures on their record, the Elite holed themselves up in their headquarters at [[Bast Castle]] on [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]]. It was there that Skywalker and his Jedi besieged them, taking the fight to the enemy. The entire dark brotherhood was slain in the encounter with the exception of their leader [[Xecr Nist]], who died in prison shortly afterward. ==History== ===Origins=== [[File:Elite1.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Darth Sidious (top) with members of his Dark Side Elite]] Following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], many of his followers believed him gone for good. However, due to his many experiments with the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], he survived, and his spirit was able to make his way to a secure [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] base on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. It was there that Sidious was [[Transfer essence|reborn]] into one of his myriad of [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] bodies that he had prepared on the dark world. Equipped with a healthy young body, Sidious made plans to retake [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]] To accomplish this task, Sidious put into motion a military strategy known as [[Operation Shadow Hand]], designed to overwhelm the enemy. To lead this grand venture, the Emperor would need brutal warriors that were totally devoted to his will. In his writing of the ''[[Dark Side Compendium]]'', Sidious had imagined a team of seven [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]. These warriors would be totally immersed in the dark side and led by the call of the Emperor.{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050512b|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 3: Pride of the Dark Side}} With his rebirth, Sidious put his dream into action. Among his many ranks of [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] found his select group of seven. He chose the Dark Jedi [[Sedriss QL|Qaga Lok]], a descendant of [[Shadow Hand]]{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}} [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], both his [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Military Executor]] and replacement for his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] as the leader of the Imperial forces. After a humbling ordeal in front of the Emperor, Lok swore his devotion to Sidious and changed his name to Sedriss QL. The rest of the group was filled out by other hand selected warriors including the fallen [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Kam Solusar]]. The rest of the Dark Side Elite included [[Vill Goir]], [[Krdys Mordi]], [[Baddon Fass]], [[Zasm Katth]], and [[Kvag Gthull]]. [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050616a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 6: Outcasts and Megalomaniacs Welcome}} [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]—who had read the ''Dark Side Compendium''— created his own corps of [[seven Dark Jedi]], which included himself. However, this group was slain by the Jedi [[Kyle Katarn]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. ===Operation Shadow Hand=== With his Dark Side cadre complete, Sidious put his plan for galactic domination in motion. Heading Operation Shadow Hand, the seven Dark Jedi brought the fight to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Unfortunately, as soon as it seemed that the Empire would be victorious on all fronts, the Emperor [[Battle of Pinnacle Base|was killed]] by Jedi Knight [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his sister, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. However, the Elite knew that their leader would return, and under Sedriss, they continued Operation Shadow Hand. Unfortunately, it was at this time that one of their number would finally come into contact with Skywalker. Kam Solusar, the son of a [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] Jedi, found Skywalker at the [[Nespis VIII|spaceport]] above the planet [[Nespis VIII]]. The [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] was able to best Solusar in a game of [[lightsider]], eventually turning him away from the dark side.[[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' [[File:Elite3.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[Baddon Fass]] leading a team of darktroopers on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]].]] The loss of Solusar became only a temporary setback as the Dark Side Elite quickly recruited another into their group, bringing their number back up to seven. With their brotherhood full again, Sedriss took Mordi and Goir to the planet [[Balmorra/Legends|Balmorra]] to secure new weapons. However, with the absence of the Emperor, the force of Sedriss' threats fell on deaf ears, and Balmorran [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Hinch Beltane|Beltane]] demanded independence from the Empire. In the following [[Battle of Balmorra|battle]], Sedriss and his men were soundly defeated by Balmorran [[X-1 Viper]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Sedriss was forced to sign a truce with Balmorra signifying their independence. The loss at Balmorra was a heavy blow to the Empire, and members of the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]] began to call for Sedriss' execution. Sedriss and four of the other Dark Side Elite were recalled to the [[Emperor's Citadel]] on Byss. Meanwhile, the remaining two Elite, Baddon Fass and Zasm Katth, piloted the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Invincible (Imperial-class)|''Invincible'']] to [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] in an attempt to capture Leia Organa Solo and her husband [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Leading [[darktrooper team two]], Fass and Katth located the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and tried to force him to work for free. The stubborn bounty hunter refused the [[darksider]]s' request and shot his way out of the encounter. However, Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spies]] were able to locate Solo's [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], and piloting their [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] over the [[City/Legends|city]], Katth and Fass tried to capture the ship in a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]]. Unfortunately, some fancy flying by Solo kept the ship from locking onto the ''Falcon'', and they instead ripped a large tower out of the city. The tower skewered the ''Invincible'', critically damaging the ship. The Star Destroyer plummeted into the city, killing Katth and Fass.[[Dark Empire II 2|''Dark Empire II'' 2]] ===Emperor's return=== {{Quote|Bring me the ''woman Jedi'' before she has her ''third child''… and set all your forces against ''Luke Skywalker!''|Darth Sidious|}} [[File:Elite2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Sedriss QL|Sedriss]] and the rest of the Dark Side Elite return to the [[Emperor's Citadel]].]] Back at the Citadel, Sedriss and the remaining Dark Side Elite confronted the Imperial Ruling Council, discovering that they had grown tired of waiting for Sidious' return. The adepts [[Nefta]] and [[Sa-Di]] had decided to ensure that the Emperor would not return and began to destroy his remaining clone bodies stored in the Citadel. Sedriss witnessed the betrayal and took it upon himself to discipline the pair. As Sedriss executed the unfaithful traitors, the Emperor had finally found his way back into another clone body. Sidious congratulated his Executor for his loyalty and assigned him with a new task. Sedriss and his men would now be responsible for capturing Skywalker and his sister.[[Star Wars Handbook 3: Dark Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 3: ''Dark Empire'']] Sedriss and the Dark Side Elite began to search for Skywalker, eventually locating Skywalker and Solusar on the planet [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. Unfortunately, at this time, Sidious received word on the fate of Fass and Katth. Calling his Military Executor into his chambers, he told Sedriss that he held him personally responsible for the deaths of two of his most powerful disciples. However, Sedriss told Sidious that he knew the location of Skywalker, and that the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] should give him one last chance. Instead of executing Sedriss, Sidious allowed him one final chance to capture Skywalker, and Sedriss and Vill Goir left for Ossus.[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]] ===Showdown=== {{Quote|Cleave their fighting arms, Goir! We'll take them ''alive!''|[[Sedriss QL|Sedriss]] to [[Vill Goir]] about [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Kam Solusar]]|[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]]}} [[File:Elite4.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Vill Goir]] and Sedriss charge the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].]] Arriving on Ossus with a group of [[darktrooper]]s[[Dark Empire II 2|''Dark Empire II'' 2]] {{1stID|Darktrooper}} onboard the [[Imperial hunter (starship)|Imperial hunter]] starship ''[[Scourge One]]'', Sedriss's men found themselves in a [[Skirmish on Ossus|conflict]] with the native [[Ysanna/Legends|Ysanna]] people. Although the Ysanna were only equipped with primitive weapons, they were led into battle by Skywalker and Kam Solusar. The Jedi's power in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] allowed them to overcome the Dark Side Elite's men, and Sedriss and Goir were forced to continue the fight by themselves. However, Sedriss and Goir were outmatched by Skywalker's power. Goir was immediately cut in half by Solusar, and Sedriss was easily disarmed by Skywalker. In a final standoff in which Sedriss tried to take [[Jem Ysanna]] hostage, the Military Executor was obliterated by the ancient Jedi [[Ood Bnar]] who was in hibernation on Ossus. News of the death of his Military Executor reached Sidious on Byss where he was overseeing the completion of his latest [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], the [[Galaxy Gun]]. Infuriated by the turn of events, the Emperor recruited two of his Dark Side Adepts, [[Xecr Nist]] and [[Tedryn-Sha]] as replacements to bring the Dark Side Elite back up to seven. To replace Sedriss, Nist was appointed as the new Imperial Executor. In a fury over the inability of his forces to locate Leia Organa or her children—whom the Emperor wanted to turn to the dark side—Sidious brought his two new Dark Side Warriors to their headquarters at [[Bast Castle]]. After arriving, both the Sith Lord and his Dark Jedi witnessed Kvag Gthull and another Dark Side Elite [[Torture/Legends|torture]] the whereabouts of the Jedi out of a captured [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Ntthan]]. Although the New Republic agent put up a good fight, he finally revealed the Jedi's presence on the safeworld of [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]]. Boarding their personal [[I-7 Howlrunner]]s, Nist and the six other Dark Side Warriors left their base in preparation for the end of the Jedi Knights. ===Second conflict=== {{Quote|By the darkness that I am, the time I have longed for has ''finally come''. The ''end'' of the Jedi Knights…I can ''taste'' it! There will be no one left to oppose my ''will!''|Darth Sidious|}} [[File:Elite6.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Rayf Ysanna]] [[Cho sun/Legends|disarms]] a member of the Dark Side Elite during the [[Battle of New Alderaan (Operation Shadow Hand)|battle of New Alderaan]].]] Arriving alongside an Imperial force ready to level the base, Nist and his warriors went ahead to capture and kill the Jedi, holding a group of advanced [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]] in reserve. Flying in low and slipping under the cover of night, the Dark Side Elite infiltrated the camp, finding the Rebels asleep and unaware. In order to neutralize Skywalker, the warriors released a group of [[Scarab Mark VI assassin droid]]s which quickly attacked and poisoned the Jedi Master. Unfortunately, just as Tedryn-Sha and Mordi prepared to capture the wounded Skywalker, Leia Organa Solo and Jem Ysanna became aware of the presence of the dark warriors and attacked. Although Sha was able to kill Jem, the quick thinking of Organa Solo found the two darksiders dead on the floor. Meanwhile, Nist and the remaining Dark Jedi sought out Leia's children, [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]] and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina]], with Gthull finally finding them. Unfortunately for the Dark Side Elite, the entire base was by now awake, determined to keep the darksiders from accomplishing their goal. As a [[Battle of New Alderaan (Operation Shadow Hand)|battle]] ensued, the Jedi [[Rayf Ysanna]] and [[Empatojayos Brand]] joined the fight. The battle resulted in the deaths of two more of the dark warriors, including the last of the original seven Dark Jedi in Kvag Gthull. Knowing that the battle was lost, and with the imminent arrival of Imperial troops, Nist and his two remaining Dark Jedi retreated to their Howlrunners. Luckily for the New Republic, Jedi [[Vima-Da-Boda]] was able to heal Skywalker of the poison introduced into his system by the Scarab droids. As the Imperial AT-ATs advanced, Skywalker and his fellow Jedi were rescued by a group of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and an [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-Wing]] [[Flight group/Legends|flight group]]. Nist and the two Dark Jedi tried to pursue the fleeing smugglers in their Howlrunners, but the latter managed to escape by making a jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. ===Last encounter=== {{Quote|It will be done, majesty. We will leave ''at once'' to capture these worthy specimens.|[[Xecr Nist]]|}} [[File:Elite8.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Rayf Ysanna encounters a member of the Dark Side Elite in [[Bast Castle]].]] Returning to the Emperor's Citadel, Nist and his remaining two Dark Jedi found that their master was more concerned for his declining health than he was for their ultimate failure at New Alderaan. Sidious quickly replenished his Elite back to seven warriors and prepared a new assignment for his Dark Jedi. After learning from his [[Emperor's physician|physician]] that his clones were deteriorating at a rapid rate, he knew that he needed to introduce genetic material from a Jedi bloodline to restore the health of his clone's genetic make-up. In this capacity, the Emperor sent his Dark Jedi to Ossus to capture members of the Ysanna tribe. Arriving on Ossus in two Imperial hunters, Nist and his men quickly rounded up three Ysanna [[Shaman/Legends|shamans]] including Chief [[Okko]]. Bringing the shamans back to Bast Castle, Nist and his men [[Carbon-freezing/Legends|froze]] them in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] so that they could be used in a new batch of clones that would be prepared on Vjun. However, just as the Dark Side Elite were settling in, a large explosion rocked the front gate of the castle, creating an opening into the Dark Jedi's headquarters. Riding in the starship ''[[Jedi Explorer II]]'', Skywalker alongside Empatojayos Brand, Kam Solusar, and Rayf Ysanna were ready to end the threat of the Dark Side Elite once and for all. Caught by surprise, Rayf and Brand easily killed two of the darksiders, and Solusar found himself in a fight for his life in a three on one battle. However, as Skywalker easily overpowered Nist in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]], he found the time to throw a [[Force lightning/Legends|ball of lightning]] at a statue of Darth Vader—which had been erected by the late Sedriss—causing it to fall on top of the Elite facing Solusar. As the [[Mission to Vjun (Operation Shadow Hand)|fight]] drew to a close, Rayf was able to finish the last darksider and Skywalker took Nist captive. ===Aftermath and legacy=== {{Quote|You may imprison me, Skywalker…but the Emperor is one who ''cannot'' be defeated.''"
"''On the contrary, Nist. In the name of ''all'' the Jedi, ''I'' am the ''one'' who ''will'' defeat him!|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and Xecr Nist|}} Despite Nist's cryptic decree, the New Republic was finally able to [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|kill]] Sidious on the planet [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] after Han Solo killed his final clone body and Empatojayos Brand trapped his spirit causing it to fade into [[Chaos/Legends|Chaos]]. Following Sidious' final death, Xecr Nist died in [[Prison/Legends|prison]], leaving Kam Solusar as the only survivor of the Dark Side Elite. With the Emperor dead, new members of the brotherhood were unable to be appointed, which meant that the Dark Side Elite were finally dead. ==Equipment== [[File:Elite7.jpg|thumb|right|225px|Sidious watches as his Dark Jedi depart Bast Castle for New Alderaan in their [[I-7 Howlrunner]]s.]] Darth Sidious' Dark Jedi were made up of Dark Side Adepts that already had minor training in the Force. Each of the Dark Jedi were equipped with long dark robes, and with the exception of Sedriss, they wore black coverings over their heads. To fill out their wardrobe, the Dark Side Elite wore dark leather gauntlets and heavy leather boots. Additionally, each Dark Side Warrior had a red-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and an I-7 Howlrunner as their personal ship. The seven Howlrunners together formed [[Dark Side Squadron]]. When Kam Solusar defected, he took his Howlrunner with him, eventually using it to smash a hole in Bast Castle during the final fight between Skywalker and the Dark Side Elite. The rest of the Howlrunners were presumably left at Bast Castle after their final destruction at the hands of Skywalker and his Jedi. ==Headquarters and forces== The Dark Side Elite had their base at the former getaway of Darth Vader at Bast Castle. The large structure had a landing pad large enough for each of the Dark Side Elite's I-7 Howlrunner ships as well as several Imperial hunters. Besides the large landing pad, the building also contained a torture room which isolated the prisoner from the outside world. The structure also housed a [[Carbon-freezing chamber/Legends|carbon-freezing chamber]], and apparently had the necessary resources for [[cloning tank]]s as well as the methods to genetically alter the created clones. Additionally, the castle was home to a large statue of Darth Vader erected by Sedriss after his appointment to Military Executor. Following Sedriss' death, Sidious ordered Xecr Nist to remove the statue from the castle. However, Nist delayed removing the effigy of the late [[Chosen One/Legends|Chosen One]], and ironically Skywalker used the large statue to crush several members of the Dark Side Elite during their final battle. Besides the Dark Side Elite, Bast Castle also served as the station for several groups of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] including a few teams of darktroopers. When the Dark Side Elite would leave their headquarters, they were often accompanied by these stormtroopers. During Fass and Katth's search for ''Millennium Falcon'' on Nar Shaddaa, they were in charge of the Star Destroyer ''Invincible'' as well all the forces aboard. ==Appointment== {{Quote|If you're smart, you'll ''join'' the Emperor as I have…and he'll make you as powerful as he has made ''me!''|Sedriss|}} [[File:Elite5.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Sidious empowers [[Xecr Nist]] and [[Tedryn-Sha]].]] When Sidious personally selected a recruit to fill the ranks of the Dark Side Elite, a personal ceremony would take place between the Dark Lord and the adept. In this ceremony, Sidious would empower the adept with the dark side of the Force, causing a vast increase in power inside the adept. The vast amount of power used to perform this ceremony created a disturbance in the Force that could be felt across the galaxy. This ceremony also included Sidious promoting the adept to the full rank of Dark Jedi. This ceremony could take place anywhere, and when Sidious promoted Tedryn-Sha and Xecr Nist, the procedure was performed in a transport in space above the Galaxy Gun. ==Membership== At any one time, Sidious' Dark Side Elite consisted of exactly seven Dark Jedi, with some having slightly more authority over the others. The original Elite consisted of Krdys Mordi, Kvag Gthull, Zasm Katth, Baddon Fass, Kam Solusar, the cadre's leader Executor Sedriss, and Vill Goir, who acted as Sedriss's substitute. After the defection of Solusar, another darksider was recruited to the Dark Side Elite, and two more replacements were appointed to the cadre after the deaths of Katth and Fass. The next change in the ranks occurred after Sedriss's and Goir's deaths on Ossus; Sidious replaced the two with Xecr Nist and Tedryn-Sha, giving his new Dark Jedi the posts of Executor and second in command, respectively. The Dark Side Elite then took high casualties during the mission to New Alderaan—Krdys Mordi was [[Sai tok/Legends|sliced in half]] by Jem Ysanna; Tedryn-Sha and Kvag Gthull were both killed by Organa Solo, and one more darksider met his end at the hands of Rayf Ysanna. After the events on New Alderaan, the number of Dark Jedi was yet again brought back to seven, although it was soon reduced again, this time drastically. Six darksiders were killed during the Jedi assault on Bast Castle—two by Rayf Ysanna, one by Empatojayos Brand, and three more were crushed by the statue of Vader, after Skywalker toppled it with the Force. The only survivor, Xecr Nist was taken into New Republic custody and soon died in a prison. With Sidious' death, new members could not be appointed to the Dark Side Elite, thus leaving the defected Solusar as the only surviving member of the group. ==Behind the scenes== The Dark Side Elite were created by [[Tom Veitch]] for the [[1994]]–[[1995]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] miniseries ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]''. They featured as antagonists throughout the series and the [[Empire's End 1|first issue]] of the followup miniseries, ''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]''. Since then, the organization has been discussed in several sourcebooks''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' and has featured in the audio dramas of [[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'']] and [[Empire's End (audio drama)|''Empire's End'']].[[Empire's End (audio drama)|''Empire's End'' audio drama]] The author [[Tom Veitch]] based the Dark Side Elite on such organisations as [[Wikipedia:Hassan-i Sabbah|Hassan-i Sabbah's]] [[Wikipedia:Assassins|Assassins]], [[Wikipedia:Adolf Hitler|Adolf Hitler's]] [[Wikipedia:Gestapo|Gestapo]] and even dark angels serving [[Wikipedia:Satan|Satan]].[[Star Wars Insider 159|''Star Wars Insider'' 159]] While the Dark Side Elite is no longer considered canon, a similar organization called the [[Knights of Ren]] appears in the [[Sequel trilogy|''Star Wars'' sequel trilogy]]. Much like the Dark Side Elite, the Knights of Ren are comprised of seven warriors and led by a black-cloaked figure ([[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]/[[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]]) as well as serving an empire ([[Dark Empire]]/[[First Order]] and [[Final Order]]). ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Dark Side Elite}} *[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]] *[[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Dark Empire II 2|''Dark Empire II'' 2]] *[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]] *[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]] *[[Dark Empire II 5|''Dark Empire II'' 5]] *[[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]] *[[Empire's End (audio drama)|''Empire's End'' audio drama]] *[[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]] *[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]] {{Mo}} ===Non-canon appearances=== *[[Lightsider (story)|''Lightsider'']] {{C|Cut story}} {{C|canceled}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars Handbook 3: Dark Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 3: ''Dark Empire'']] *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{FactFile|85|SED 1-4|Exekutor Sedress}} {{C|German version}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050512b|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 3: Pride of the Dark Side}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{InsiderCite|159|Death to the Dark Side!: The Making of Dark Empire – Part III|Death to the Dark Side!: The Making of ''Dark Empire'' – Part III|reprint=1}} {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Élite del Lado Oscuro }} [[Category:Dark Empire organizations]] [[Category:Sith acolytes and apprentices]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Dark Side Elite]] }}
